# restful_operation (Resource)

`restful_operation` represents a one-time API call operation.

## Schema

### Required

- `method` (String) The HTTP method for the `Create`/`Update` call.

	-> Value must be one of: ["GET" "PUT" "POST" "PATCH" "DELETE"].
- `path` (String) The path for the `Create`/`Update` call, relative to the `base_url` of the provider.

	~> If the value of this attribute changes, Terraform will destroy and recreate the resource.

### Optional

- `body` (Dynamic) The payload for the `Create`/`Update` call.
- `delete_body` (Dynamic) The payload for the `Delete` call.
- `delete_header` (Map of Strings) The header parameters that are applied to each delete request. This overrides the `header` set in the resource block.
- `delete_method` (String) The method for the `Delete` call. If this is not specified, no `Delete` call will occur.

	-> Value must be one of: ["POST" "PUT" "PATCH" "DELETE"].
- `delete_path` (String) The path for the `Delete` call, relative to the `base_url` of the provider. The `path` is used instead if `delete_path` is absent.

	-> Ensure that if an attribute is set, also these are set: "[<.delete_method]".

	-> Ensure this is a path builder expression.
- `delete_query` (Map of Lists of Strings) The query parameters that are applied to each delete request. This overrides the `query` set in the resource block.
- `ephemeral_body` (Dynamic, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) The ephemeral (write-only) properties of the resource. This will be merge-patched to the `body` to construct the actual request body.
- `header` (Map of Strings) The header parameters that are applied to each request. This overrides the `header` set in the provider block.
- `id_builder` (String) The pattern used to build the `id`. The `path` is used as the `id` instead if absent.This can be a string literal, or combined by following params: path param: `$(path)` expanded to `path`, body param: `$(body.x.y.z)` expands to the `x.y.z` property of the API body. Especially for the body param, it can add a chain of functions (applied from left to right), in the form of `$f1.f2(body)`. Supported functions include: `escape` (URL path escape, by default applied), `unescape` (URL path unescape), `query_escape` (URL query escape), `query_unescape` (URL query unescape), `base` (filepath base), `url_path` (path segment of a URL), `trim_path` (trim `path`).

	-> Ensure this is a path builder expression.
- `operation_header` (Map of Strings) The header parameters that are applied to each operation request. This overrides the `header` set in the resource block.
- `operation_query` (Map of Lists of Strings) The query parameters that are applied to each operation request. This overrides the `query` set in the resource block.
- `output_attrs` (Set of Strings) A set of `output` attribute paths (in [gjson syntax](https://github.com/tidwall/gjson/blob/master/SYNTAX.md)) that will be exported in the `output`. If this is not specified, all attributes will be exported by `output`.
- `poll` (Object) The polling option for the "`Create`/`Update`" operation See [below for nested schema](#nested--poll).
- `poll_delete` (Object) The polling option for the "`Delete`" operation See [below for nested schema](#nested--poll_delete).

	-> Ensure that if an attribute is set, also these are set: "[<.delete_method]".
- `precheck` (List of Objects) An array of prechecks that need to pass prior to the "`Create`/`Update`" operation. See [below for nested schema](#nested--precheck).
- `precheck_delete` (List of Objects) An array of prechecks that need to pass prior to the "`Delete`" operation. See [below for nested schema](#nested--precheck_delete).

	-> Ensure that if an attribute is set, also these are set: "[<.delete_method]".
- `query` (Map of Lists of Strings) The query parameters that are applied to each request. This overrides the `query` set in the provider block.
- `use_sensitive_output` (Boolean) Whether to use `sensitive_output` instead of `output`. When true, the response will be stored in `sensitive_output` (which is marked as sensitive). Defaults to `false`.

	~> If the value of this attribute changes, Terraform will destroy and recreate the resource.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of the operation.

	~> Once set, the value of this attribute in state will not change.
- `output` (Dynamic) The response body. If `ephemeral_body` get returned by API, it will be removed from `output`. This is only populated when `use_sensitive_output` is false.
- `sensitive_output` (Dynamic, Sensitive) The response body (sensitive). If `ephemeral_body` get returned by API, it will be removed from `sensitive_output`. This is only populated when `use_sensitive_output` is true.

<a id="nested--poll"></a>
### Nested Schema for `poll`

Required:

- `status` (Object) The expected status sentinels for each polling state. See [below for nested schema](#nested--poll.status).
- `status_locator` (String) Specifies how to discover the status property. The format is either `code` or `scope.path`, where `scope` can be either `header` or `body`, and the `path` is using the [gjson syntax](https://github.com/tidwall/gjson/blob/master/SYNTAX.md). The `path` can contain `$(body.x.y.z)` parameter that reference property from either the response body (for `Create`, after selector), or `state.output` (for `Read`/`Update`/`Delete`).

Optional:

- `default_delay_sec` (Int64) The interval between two pollings if there is no `Retry-After` in the response header, in second. Defaults to `10`.
- `header` (Map of Strings) The header parameters. This overrides the `header` set in the resource block.
- `url_locator` (String) Specifies how to discover the polling url. The format can be one of `header.path` (use the property at `path` in response header), `body.path` (use the property at `path` in response body) or `exact.value` (use the exact `value`). When absent, the current operation's URL is used for polling, execpt `Create` where it fallbacks to use the path constructed by the `read_path` as the polling URL.
<a id="nested--poll.status"></a>
### Nested Schema for `poll.status`

Required:

- `success` (String) The expected status sentinel for suceess status.

Optional:

- `pending` (List of Strings) The expected status sentinels for pending status.
<a id="nested--poll_delete"></a>
### Nested Schema for `poll_delete`

Required:

- `status` (Object) The expected status sentinels for each polling state. See [below for nested schema](#nested--poll_delete.status).
- `status_locator` (String) Specifies how to discover the status property. The format is either `code` or `scope.path`, where `scope` can be either `header` or `body`, and the `path` is using the [gjson syntax](https://github.com/tidwall/gjson/blob/master/SYNTAX.md). The `path` can contain `$(body.x.y.z)` parameter that reference property from either the response body (for `Create`, after selector), or `state.output` (for `Read`/`Update`/`Delete`).

Optional:

- `default_delay_sec` (Int64) The interval between two pollings if there is no `Retry-After` in the response header, in second. Defaults to `10`.
- `header` (Map of Strings) The header parameters. This overrides the `header` set in the resource block.
- `url_locator` (String) Specifies how to discover the polling url. The format can be one of `header.path` (use the property at `path` in response header), `body.path` (use the property at `path` in response body) or `exact.value` (use the exact `value`). When absent, the current operation's URL is used for polling, execpt `Create` where it fallbacks to use the path constructed by the `read_path` as the polling URL.
<a id="nested--poll_delete.status"></a>
### Nested Schema for `poll_delete.status`

Required:

- `success` (String) The expected status sentinel for suceess status.

Optional:

- `pending` (List of Strings) The expected status sentinels for pending status.
<a id="nested--precheck"></a>
### Nested Schema for `precheck`

Optional:

- `api` (Object) Keeps waiting until the specified API meets the success status See [below for nested schema](#nested--precheck.api).

	-> Ensure that one and only one attribute from this collection is set: "[<.api,<.mutex]".
- `mutex` (String) The name of the mutex, which implies the resource will keep waiting until this mutex is held

	-> Ensure that one and only one attribute from this collection is set: "[<.mutex,<.api]".
<a id="nested--precheck.api"></a>
### Nested Schema for `precheck.api`

Required:

- `path` (String) The path used to query readiness, relative to the `base_url` of the provider.
- `status` (Object) The expected status sentinels for each polling state. See [below for nested schema](#nested--precheck.api.status).
- `status_locator` (String) Specifies how to discover the status property. The format is either `code` or `scope.path`, where `scope` can be either `header` or `body`, and the `path` is using the [gjson syntax](https://github.com/tidwall/gjson/blob/master/SYNTAX.md).

Optional:

- `default_delay_sec` (Int64) The interval between two pollings if there is no `Retry-After` in the response header, in second. Defaults to `10`.
- `header` (Map of Strings) The header parameters. This overrides the `header` set in the resource block.
- `query` (Map of Lists of Strings) The query parameters. This overrides the `query` set in the resource block.
<a id="nested--precheck.api.status"></a>
### Nested Schema for `precheck.api.status`

Required:

- `success` (String) The expected status sentinel for suceess status.

Optional:

- `pending` (List of Strings) The expected status sentinels for pending status.
<a id="nested--precheck_delete"></a>
### Nested Schema for `precheck_delete`

Optional:

- `api` (Object) Keeps waiting until the specified API meets the success status See [below for nested schema](#nested--precheck_delete.api).

	-> Ensure that one and only one attribute from this collection is set: "[<.api,<.mutex]".
- `mutex` (String) The name of the mutex, which implies the resource will keep waiting until this mutex is held

	-> Ensure that one and only one attribute from this collection is set: "[<.mutex,<.api]".
<a id="nested--precheck_delete.api"></a>
### Nested Schema for `precheck_delete.api`

Required:

- `status` (Object) The expected status sentinels for each polling state. See [below for nested schema](#nested--precheck_delete.api.status).
- `status_locator` (String) Specifies how to discover the status property. The format is either `code` or `scope.path`, where `scope` can be either `header` or `body`, and the `path` is using the [gjson syntax](https://github.com/tidwall/gjson/blob/master/SYNTAX.md). The `path` can contain `$(body.x.y.z)` parameter that reference property from the `state.output`.

Optional:

- `default_delay_sec` (Int64) The interval between two pollings if there is no `Retry-After` in the response header, in second. Defaults to `10`.
- `header` (Map of Strings) The header parameters. This overrides the `header` set in the resource block.
- `path` (String) The path used to query readiness, relative to the `base_url` of the provider. By default, the `path` of this resource is used.
- `query` (Map of Lists of Strings) The query parameters. This overrides the `query` set in the resource block.

### Nested Schema for `precheck_delete.api.status`

Required:

- `success` (String) The expected status sentinel for suceess status.

Optional:

- `pending` (List of Strings) The expected status sentinels for pending status.
